page: Vultr vhf-16c-58gb is a High Frequency Compute server configured with 16 shared vCPUs mapped to 8 physical Intel x86_64 cores. It features 58.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 3.62 GB per core, and includes 1024 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, supported by a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1d, 4 MB L2, and 16 MB L3 cache. It supports hardware-level features such as Advanced Vector Extensions, AES cryptography, and Hyper-Threading.

Benchmark evaluations show average performance across most standard compute and memory metrics. The server achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1884.79 and a multi-core score of 14058.95. Single-threaded gzip compression is a notable strength, reaching 30.89 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth for large, uncached blocks is limited, with read speeds at 24.85 GB/sec and write speeds at 13.66 GB/sec. Static web serving throughput is also relatively weak at 6.08 GB/sec, while database operations and Redis SET performance remain in the average tier.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this server offers a cost-effective option for workloads requiring high resource density and substantial local storage without needing dedicated CPU cores. The combination of 58.0 GB RAM and a 1024 GB NVMe SSD makes it suitable for database operations, general-purpose web serving, and multi-threaded file decompression tasks where occasional CPU sharing does not impact baseline operations.
description: Vultr vhf-16c-58gb is a High Frequency Compute server configured with 16 shared Intel x86_64 vCPUs, 58.0 GB of RAM, and 1024 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating at 2.0 GHz, the processor supports AVX and AES instructions. Benchmark results show average multi-core compute performance with a stress-ng score of 14058.95 and strong single-threaded gzip compression at 30.89 GB/sec, though uncached memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput are weaker. This configuration is well-suited for database hosting, general-purpose applications, and file processing tasks that benefit from high storage capacity and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratios under a shared CPU model.
og_description: Vultr vhf-16c-58gb is a High Frequency Compute server featuring 16 shared Intel vCPUs, 58.0 GB of RAM, and 1024 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, offering balanced general-purpose and database performance.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-16c-58gb features 16 shared Intel vCPUs, 58.0 GB RAM, and 1024 GB NVMe SSD storage for general-purpose and database workloads.
tagline: A shared-CPU server balancing sixteen vCPUs, fifty-eight gigabytes of memory, and one terabyte of local NVMe SSD storage.
